methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Argotec, a growth-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by David Avino, is targeting €75M in this financing round. The valuation universe comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace—a total of 13 datapoints across the sector.

Two standard triangulation methods were evaluated. Peer-median analysis proved inapplicable: while 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, the narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, similar-valuation-tier) yielded fewer than three datapoints, making a subset median unreliable. An all-sector fallback would introduce unacceptable noise. Public-comp anchoring was similarly constrained: the Space & Aerospace public median trades at 64.2x revenue across the six tickers, but Argotec's revenue is not on file, preventing P/S application.

The data does not support a specific valuation range or synthesis at this time. The absence of both sufficient same-stage private peer financials and the target's own revenue figure eliminates the primary quantitative paths forward. A defensible valuation would require either disclosure of Argotec's revenue, access to recent funding data from the identified private peer set, or alternative metrics (EBITDA multiples, discounted cash flow inputs) not present in the current dataset. Additional diligence is necessary before proceeding to a point estimate.
